Python Async Programming: Asynchronous programming in Python uses the asyncio library and the async/await syntax to write concurrent code without threads. Event loops manage coroutines, allowing I/O-bound tasks like HTTP requests and database queries to run concurrently without blocking. Libraries like aiohttp, httpx, and asyncpg provide async-native interfaces for common I/O operations. Understanding the difference between concurrency and parallelism is essential before choosing between asyncio, threading, and multiprocessing.
